justification: All 6 ACs correctly implemented. AC3 handles both FAILED and DECLINED. AC4 uses logger.warn (not logger.error) and returns unknown. AC5 uses toFixed(2)+parseFloat for correct decimal handling (1099->10.99). AC6 uses != null guard covering both null and undefined. 16 tests cover all 6 ACs with explicit names; AC3 tested with both inputs; AC4 verifies logger.warn called. acceptance_criteria_coverage maps all 6 ACs. Logger is injected as a parameter, not global. No external dependencies.

observations.text: AC3 explicitly tests both FAILED and DECLINED inputs
AC4 correctly uses logger.warn, not logger.error, and warn message includes raw status value and order id
AC5 uses toFixed(2)+parseFloat to avoid floating-point drift, tested with multiple amount cases
AC6 uses != null guard which correctly catches both undefined and null
Logger injected as function parameter, not a global dependency
16 tests organized by AC with explicit descriptive names
Bonus: tests edge cases like empty string status, lowercase status, zero cents
